1363	class ConnectionPool(ConnectionPoolInterface):
1364	    """
1365	    Create a connection pool. ``If max_connections`` is set, then this
1366	    object raises :py:class:`~redis.ConnectionError` when the pool's
1367	    limit is reached.
1368	
1369	    By default, TCP connections are created unless ``connection_class``
1370	    is specified. Use :py:class:`~redis.UnixDomainSocketConnection` for
1371	    unix sockets.
1372	    :py:class:`~redis.SSLConnection` can be used for SSL enabled connections.
1373	
1374	    Any additional keyword arguments are passed to the constructor of
1375	    ``connection_class``.
1376	    """
1377	
1378	    @classmethod
1379	    def from_url(cls: Type[_CP], url: str, **kwargs) -> _CP:
1380	        """
1381	        Return a connection pool configured from the given URL.
1382	
1383	        For example::
1384	
1385	            redis://[[username]:[password]]@localhost:6379/0
1386	            rediss://[[username]:[password]]@localhost:6379/0
1387	            unix://[username@]/path/to/socket.sock?db=0[&password=password]
1388	
1389	        Three URL schemes are supported:
1390	
1391	        - `redis://` creates a TCP socket connection. See more at:
1392	          <https://www.iana.org/assignments/uri-schemes/prov/redis>
1393	        - `rediss://` creates a SSL wrapped TCP socket connection. See more at:
1394	          <https://www.iana.org/assignments/uri-schemes/prov/rediss>
1395	        - ``unix://``: creates a Unix Domain Socket connection.
1396	
1397	        The username, password, hostname, path and all querystring values
1398	        are passed through urllib.parse.unquote in order to replace any
1399	        percent-encoded values with their corresponding characters.
1400	
1401	        There are several ways to specify a database number. The first value
1402	        found will be used:
1403	
1404	        1. A ``db`` querystring option, e.g. redis://localhost?db=0
1405	
1406	        2. If using the redis:// or rediss:// schemes, the path argument
1407	               of the url, e.g. redis://localhost/0
1408	
1409	        3. A ``db`` keyword argument to this function.
1410	
1411	        If none of these options are specified, the default db=0 is used.
1412	
1413	        All querystring options are cast to their appropriate Python types.
1414	        Boolean arguments can be specified with string values "True"/"False"
1415	        or "Yes"/"No". Values that cannot be properly cast cause a
1416	        ``ValueError`` to be raised. Once parsed, the querystring arguments
1417	        and keyword arguments are passed to the ``ConnectionPool``'s
1418	        class initializer. In the case of conflicting arguments, querystring
1419	        arguments always win.
1420	        """
1421	        url_options = parse_url(url)
1422	        kwargs.update(url_options)
1423	        return cls(**kwargs)
1424	
1425	    def __init__(
1426	        self,
1427	        connection_class: Type[AbstractConnection] = Connection,
1428	        max_connections: Optional[int] = None,
1429	        **connection_kwargs,
1430	    ):
1431	        max_connections = max_connections or 100
1432	        if not isinstance(max_connections, int) or max_connections < 0:
1433	            raise ValueError('"max_connections" must be a positive integer')
1434	
1435	        self.connection_class = connection_class
1436	        self.connection_kwargs = connection_kwargs
1437	        self.max_connections = max_connections
1438	
1439	        self._available_connections: List[AbstractConnection] = []
1440	        self._in_use_connections: Set[AbstractConnection] = set()
1441	        self.encoder_class = self.connection_kwargs.get("encoder_class", Encoder)
1442	        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
1443	        self._event_dispatcher = self.connection_kwargs.get("event_dispatcher", None)
1444	        if self._event_dispatcher is None:
1445	            self._event_dispatcher = EventDispatcher()
1446	
1447	    # Keys that should be redacted in __repr__ to avoid exposing sensitive information
1448	    SENSITIVE_REPR_KEYS = frozenset(
1449	        {
1450	            "password",
1451	            "username",
1452	            "ssl_password",
1453	            "credential_provider",
1454	        }
1455	    )
1456	
1457	    def __repr__(self):
1458	        conn_kwargs = ",".join(
1459	            [
1460	                f"{k}={'<REDACTED>' if k in self.SENSITIVE_REPR_KEYS else v}"
1461	                for k, v in self.connection_kwargs.items()
1462	            ]
1463	        )
1464	        return (
1465	            f"<{self.__class__.__module__}.{self.__class__.__name__}"
1466	            f"(<{self.connection_class.__module__}.{self.connection_class.__name__}"
1467	            f"({conn_kwargs})>)>"
1468	        )
1469	
1470	    def get_protocol(self):
1471	        """
1472	        Returns:
1473	            The RESP protocol version, or ``None`` if the protocol is not specified,
1474	            in which case the server default will be used.
1475	        """
1476	        return self.connection_kwargs.get("protocol", None)
1477	
1478	    def reset(self):
1479	        # Record metrics for connections being removed before clearing
1480	        # (only if attributes exist - they won't during __init__)
1481	        if hasattr(self, "_available_connections") and hasattr(
1482	            self, "_in_use_connections"
1483	        ):
1484	            idle_count = len(self._available_connections)
1485	            in_use_count = len(self._in_use_connections)
1486	            if idle_count > 0 or in_use_count > 0:
1487	                pool_name = get_pool_name(self)
1488	                # Note: Using sync version since reset() is sync
1489	                from redis.observability.recorder import (
1490	                    record_connection_count as sync_record_connection_count,
1491	                )
1492	
1493	                if idle_count > 0:
1494	                    sync_record_connection_count(
1495	                        pool_name=pool_name,
1496	                        connection_state=ConnectionState.IDLE,
1497	                        counter=-idle_count,
1498	                    )
1499	                if in_use_count > 0:
1500	                    sync_record_connection_count(
1501	                        pool_name=pool_name,
1502	                        connection_state=ConnectionState.USED,
1503	                        counter=-in_use_count,
1504	                    )
1505	
1506	        self._available_connections = []
1507	        self._in_use_connections = weakref.WeakSet()
1508	
1509	    def __del__(self) -> None:
1510	        """Clean up connection pool and record metrics when garbage collected."""
1511	        try:
1512	            if not hasattr(self, "_available_connections") or not hasattr(
1513	                self, "_in_use_connections"
1514	            ):
1515	                return
1516	            idle_count = len(self._available_connections)
1517	            in_use_count = len(self._in_use_connections)
1518	            if idle_count > 0 or in_use_count > 0:
1519	                pool_name = get_pool_name(self)
1520	                # Note: Using sync version since __del__ is sync
1521	                from redis.observability.recorder import (
1522	                    record_connection_count as sync_record_connection_count,
1523	                )
1524	
1525	                if idle_count > 0:
1526	                    sync_record_connection_count(
1527	                        pool_name=pool_name,
1528	                        connection_state=ConnectionState.IDLE,
1529	                        counter=-idle_count,
1530	                    )
1531	                if in_use_count > 0:
1532	                    sync_record_connection_count(
1533	                        pool_name=pool_name,
1534	                        connection_state=ConnectionState.USED,
1535	                        counter=-in_use_count,
1536	                    )
1537	        except Exception:
1538	            pass
1539	
1540	    def can_get_connection(self) -> bool:
1541	        """Return True if a connection can be retrieved from the pool."""
1542	        return (
1543	            self._available_connections
1544	            or len(self._in_use_connections) < self.max_connections
1545	        )
1546	
1547	    @deprecated_args(
1548	        args_to_warn=["*"],
1549	        reason="Use get_connection() without args instead",
1550	        version="5.3.0",
1551	    )
1552	    async def get_connection(self, command_name=None, *keys, **options):
1553	        """Get a connected connection from the pool"""
1554	        # Track connection count before to detect if a new connection is created
1555	        async with self._lock:
1556	            connections_before = len(self._available_connections) + len(
1557	                self._in_use_connections
1558	            )
1559	            start_time_created = time.monotonic()
1560	            connection = self.get_available_connection()
1561	            connections_after = len(self._available_connections) + len(
1562	                self._in_use_connections
1563	            )
1564	            is_created = connections_after > connections_before
1565	
1566	        # Record state transition for observability
1567	        # This ensures counters stay balanced if ensure_connection() fails and release() is called
1568	        pool_name = get_pool_name(self)
1569	        if is_created:
1570	            # New connection created and acquired: just USED +1
1571	            await record_connection_count(
1572	                pool_name=pool_name,
1573	                connection_state=ConnectionState.USED,
1574	                counter=1,
1575	            )
1576	        else:
1577	            # Existing connection acquired from pool: IDLE -> USED
1578	            await record_connection_count(
1579	                pool_name=pool_name,
1580	                connection_state=ConnectionState.IDLE,
1581	                counter=-1,
1582	            )
1583	            await record_connection_count(
1584	                pool_name=pool_name,
1585	                connection_state=ConnectionState.USED,
1586	                counter=1,
1587	            )
1588	
1589	        # We now perform the connection check outside of the lock.
1590	        try:
1591	            await self.ensure_connection(connection)
1592	
1593	            if is_created:
1594	                await record_connection_create_time(
1595	                    connection_pool=self,
1596	                    duration_seconds=time.monotonic() - start_time_created,
1597	                )
1598	
1599	            return connection
1600	        except BaseException:
1601	            await self.release(connection)
1602	            raise
1603	
1604	    def get_available_connection(self):
1605	        """Get a connection from the pool, without making sure it is connected"""
1606	        try:
1607	            connection = self._available_connections.pop()
1608	        except IndexError:
1609	            if len(self._in_use_connections) >= self.max_connections:
1610	                raise MaxConnectionsError("Too many connections") from None
1611	            connection = self.make_connection()
1612	        self._in_use_connections.add(connection)
1613	        return connection
1614	
1615	    def get_encoder(self):
1616	        """Return an encoder based on encoding settings"""
1617	        kwargs = self.connection_kwargs
1618	        return self.encoder_class(
1619	            encoding=kwargs.get("encoding", "utf-8"),
1620	            encoding_errors=kwargs.get("encoding_errors", "strict"),
1621	            decode_responses=kwargs.get("decode_responses", False),
1622	        )
1623	
1624	    def make_connection(self):
1625	        """Create a new connection.  Can be overridden by child classes."""
1626	        # Note: We don't record IDLE here because async uses a sync make_connection
1627	        # but async record_connection_count. The recording is handled in get_connection.
1628	        return self.connection_class(**self.connection_kwargs)
1629	
1630	    async def ensure_connection(self, connection: AbstractConnection):
1631	        """Ensure that the connection object is connected and valid"""
1632	        await connection.connect()
1633	        # connections that the pool provides should be ready to send
1634	        # a command. if not, the connection was either returned to the
1635	        # pool before all data has been read or the socket has been
1636	        # closed. either way, reconnect and verify everything is good.
1637	        try:
1638	            if await connection.can_read():
1639	                raise ConnectionError("Connection has data") from None
1640	        except (ConnectionError, TimeoutError, OSError):
1641	            await connection.disconnect()
1642	            await connection.connect()
1643	            if await connection.can_read():
1644	                raise ConnectionError("Connection not ready") from None
1645	
1646	    async def release(self, connection: AbstractConnection):
1647	        """Releases the connection back to the pool"""
1648	        # Connections should always be returned to the correct pool,
1649	        # not doing so is an error that will cause an exception here.
1650	        self._in_use_connections.remove(connection)
1651	
1652	        if connection.should_reconnect():
1653	            await connection.disconnect()
1654	
1655	        self._available_connections.append(connection)
1656	        await self._event_dispatcher.dispatch_async(
1657	            AsyncAfterConnectionReleasedEvent(connection)
1658	        )
1659	
1660	        # Record state transition: USED -> IDLE
1661	        pool_name = get_pool_name(self)
1662	        await record_connection_count(
1663	            pool_name=pool_name,
1664	            connection_state=ConnectionState.USED,
1665	            counter=-1,
1666	        )
1667	        await record_connection_count(
1668	            pool_name=pool_name,
1669	            connection_state=ConnectionState.IDLE,
1670	            counter=1,
1671	        )
1672	
1673	    async def disconnect(self, inuse_connections: bool = True):
1674	        """
1675	        Disconnects connections in the pool
1676	
1677	        If ``inuse_connections`` is True, disconnect connections that are
1678	        current in use, potentially by other tasks. Otherwise only disconnect
1679	        connections that are idle in the pool.
1680	        """
1681	        if inuse_connections:
1682	            connections: Iterable[AbstractConnection] = chain(
1683	                self._available_connections, self._in_use_connections
1684	            )
1685	        else:
1686	            connections = self._available_connections
1687	        resp = await asyncio.gather(
1688	            *(connection.disconnect() for connection in connections),
1689	            return_exceptions=True,
1690	        )
1691	
1692	        exc = next((r for r in resp if isinstance(r, BaseException)), None)
1693	        if exc:
1694	            raise exc
1695	
1696	    async def update_active_connections_for_reconnect(self):
1697	        """
1698	        Mark all active connections for reconnect.
1699	        """
1700	        async with self._lock:
1701	            for conn in self._in_use_connections:
1702	                conn.mark_for_reconnect()
1703	
1704	    async def aclose(self) -> None:
1705	        """Close the pool, disconnecting all connections"""
1706	        await self.disconnect()
1707	
1708	    async def __aenter__(self: _CP) -> _CP:
1709	        return self
1710	
1711	    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback) -> None:
1712	        await self.aclose()
1713	
1714	    def set_retry(self, retry: "Retry") -> None:
1715	        for conn in self._available_connections:
1716	            conn.retry = retry
1717	        for conn in self._in_use_connections:
1718	            conn.retry = retry
1719	
1720	    async def re_auth_callback(self, token: TokenInterface):
1721	        async with self._lock:
1722	            for conn in self._available_connections:
1723	                await conn.retry.call_with_retry(
1724	                    lambda: conn.send_command(
1725	                        "AUTH", token.try_get("oid"), token.get_value()
1726	                    ),
1727	                    lambda error: self._mock(error),
1728	                )
1729	                await conn.retry.call_with_retry(
1730	                    lambda: conn.read_response(), lambda error: self._mock(error)
1731	                )
1732	            for conn in self._in_use_connections:
1733	                conn.set_re_auth_token(token)
1734	
1735	    async def _mock(self, error: RedisError):
1736	        """
1737	        Dummy functions, needs to be passed as error callback to retry object.
1738	        :param error:
1739	        :return:
1740	        """
1741	        pass
1742	
1743	    def get_connection_count(self) -> List[tuple[int, dict]]:
1744	        """
1745	        Returns a connection count (both idle and in use).
1746	        """
1747	        attributes = AttributeBuilder.build_base_attributes()
1748	        attributes[DB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_POOL_NAME] = get_pool_name(self)
1749	        free_connections_attributes = attributes.copy()
1750	        in_use_connections_attributes = attributes.copy()
1751	
1752	        free_connections_attributes[DB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_STATE] = (
1753	            ConnectionState.IDLE.value
1754	        )
1755	        in_use_connections_attributes[DB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_STATE] = (
1756	            ConnectionState.USED.value
1757	        )
1758	
1759	        return [
1760	            (len(self._available_connections), free_connections_attributes),
1761	            (len(self._in_use_connections), in_use_connections_attributes),
1762	        ]

1765	class BlockingConnectionPool(ConnectionPool):
1766	    """
1767	    A blocking connection pool::
1768	
1769	        >>> from redis.asyncio import Redis, BlockingConnectionPool
1770	        >>> client = Redis.from_pool(BlockingConnectionPool())
1771	
1772	    It performs the same function as the default
1773	    :py:class:`~redis.asyncio.ConnectionPool` implementation, in that,
1774	    it maintains a pool of reusable connections that can be shared by
1775	    multiple async redis clients.
1776	
1777	    The difference is that, in the event that a client tries to get a
1778	    connection from the pool when all of connections are in use, rather than
1779	    raising a :py:class:`~redis.ConnectionError` (as the default
1780	    :py:class:`~redis.asyncio.ConnectionPool` implementation does), it
1781	    blocks the current `Task` for a specified number of seconds until
1782	    a connection becomes available.
1783	
1784	    Use ``max_connections`` to increase / decrease the pool size::
1785	
1786	        >>> pool = BlockingConnectionPool(max_connections=10)
1787	
1788	    Use ``timeout`` to tell it either how many seconds to wait for a connection
1789	    to become available, or to block forever:
1790	
1791	        >>> # Block forever.
1792	        >>> pool = BlockingConnectionPool(timeout=None)
1793	
1794	        >>> # Raise a ``ConnectionError`` after five seconds if a connection is
1795	        >>> # not available.
1796	        >>> pool = BlockingConnectionPool(timeout=5)
1797	    """
1798	
1799	    def __init__(
1800	        self,

1810	            **connection_kwargs,

1813	        self.timeout = timeout

1819	    )

input.prompt:
You are writing hermetic async tests for a maintenance-notifications feature being added (by someone else, in parallel) to the async standalone client of a modified redis-py repo at /private/var/folders/hj/_j2mx6hd7_b1_pnjzwkktlsr0000gn/T/sidekick-redis-async-maint-notifications-rktgawgv (work only inside it; NOT a git repo — no git commands).

READ FIRST, in this order:
1. .async_maint_contract.md at the repo root — the binding API contract for the async implementation you are testing. Write tests strictly against this contract; the implementation does not exist yet, so import errors when running against today's code are EXPECTED (that is the proof your tests fail on today's code).
2. tests/maint_notifications/test_maint_notifications.py and tests/maint_notifications/test_maint_notifications_handling.py — the house wire-level testing style you must mirror (scripted sockets, no live server, @pytest.mark.fixed_client on test classes, docstrings on tests).
3. redis/maint_notifications.py (shared notification/config classes — reused by async, do NOT re-test them), redis/asyncio/connection.py, redis/_parsers/base.py lines 405-500 (AsyncPushNotificationsParser + _AsyncRESPBase.on_connect/can_read — note the parser reads from connection._reader, a real asyncio.StreamReader, and can_read() inspects self._stream._buffer and at_eof()).
4. pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options]: asyncio_mode = "auto", so plain `async def test_*` functions work without decorators.

YOUR FILE BOUNDARY (create ONLY these; touch nothing else):
- tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications.py
- tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications_handling.py
- tests/maint_notifications/async_mock_server.py (shared mock-stream helper for the two files, if you want one — recommended)

MOCK TRANSPORT DESIGN (the async analogue of the sync MockSocket in test_maint_notifications_handling.py):
- Patch `asyncio.open_connection` (redis.asyncio.connection.Connection._connect calls it with host=..., port=...). Use unittest.mock.patch("asyncio.open_connection", side_effect=factory).
- For each opened connection create a real asyncio.StreamReader plus a MagicMock writer. The writer mock must implement: write(data)/writelines(chunks) which run the same command→response scripting logic as the sync MockSocket.send (HELLO → RESP3 hello map; CLIENT MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS + b"internal-ip" → b"+ERROR\r\n"; SET with key_receive_migrating/migrated/failing_over/failed_over/moving/moving_none patterns → push frame prepended to +OK; GET variants; default +OK) and immediately `reader.feed_data(response_bytes)`; an async drain() no-op; close() marking the fake closed + reader.feed_eof(); async wait_closed(); and `transport.get_extra_info("socket")` returning a fake socket object exposing getpeername() -> (host, port), getsockname() -> (host, 12345), settimeout/gettimeout, plus a `connected`/`closed` flag so tests can count live sockets. Track every created mock (list on the test class) and record the (host, port) each open_connection call targeted so tests can assert reconnection went to the moved endpoint.
- Reuse the exact push frame bytes from the sync suite (e.g. >3\r\n$9\r\nMIGRATING\r\n:1\r\n:10\r\n, MOVING frame with ttl and "1.2.3.4:6379", and the null-endpoint MOVING variant with `_\r\n`), and the same constants AFTER_MOVING_ADDRESS="1.2.3.4:6379", DEFAULT_ADDRESS="12.45.34.56:6379", MOVING_TIMEOUT=1.
- No live server, no real sockets, no sleeps longer than ~1.5s (MOVING ttl expiry tests may `await asyncio.sleep(MOVING_TIMEOUT + 0.3)`).

COVERAGE — test_async_maint_notifications.py (unit level, Mock/AsyncMock-based, mirroring the sync TestMaintNotificationsPoolHandler / TestMaintNotificationsConnectionHandler classes but importing the async handlers from redis.asyncio.maint_notifications):
- Pool handler: init attrs (asyncio.Lock, shared _processed_notifications via get_handler_for_connection), remove_expired_notifications with patched time.monotonic, handle_notification dispatch (moving → handle_node_moving_notification awaited; unknown type → no-op), disabled config (proactive_reconnect=False, relaxed_timeout=-1) → returns without processing, already-processed notification → skipped, successful moving flow (pool AsyncMock: update_connections_settings awaited once with the contract kwargs; update_connection_kwargs called with MOVING state/hash/host/relaxed timeouts; notification added to processed set), moving with new_node_host None → host NOT in update_connection_kwargs and deferred proactive reconnect scheduled, handle_node_moved_notification reverts kwargs when hash matches and calls update_connections_settings with reset flags.
- Connection handler: dispatch of MIGRATING/FAILING_OVER → handle_maintenance_start_notification with MaintenanceState.MAINTENANCE; MIGRATED/FAILED_OVER → handle_maintenance_completed_notification; moving-state and relaxed-disabled guards no-op; success paths set connection.maintenance_state, call set_tmp_settings(tmp_relaxed_timeout=...), update_current_socket_timeout(...) — use a plain Mock connection whose maintenance_state starts at MaintenanceState.NONE.

COVERAGE — test_async_maint_notifications_handling.py (wire level, redis.asyncio.Redis + async ConnectionPool/BlockingConnectionPool over the mock streams):
- Handshake: enabled=True + EXTERNAL_IP endpoint → set/get succeed and sent bytes contain b"MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS" after b"HELLO"; enabled="auto" + INTERNAL_IP (mock replies +ERROR) → operations still succeed; enabled=True + INTERNAL_IP → ResponseError raised.
- Client init: redis.asyncio.Redis(protocol=3, maint_notifications_config=cfg) — pool connection_kwargs carry maint_notifications_pool_handler; a connection from the pool has parser.node_moving_push_handler_func / maintenance_push_handler_func bound to per-connection handler copies sharing the pool handler's _processed_notifications and _lock; orig_host_address/orig_socket_timeout populated.
- update_maint_notifications_config on a pool created with enabled=False: existing connection gains handlers afterwards (await it).
- Both pool classes (parametrize ConnectionPool/BlockingConnectionPool) for: basic ops; MIGRATING → in-flight connection's socket_timeout relaxed to config value (assert connection.socket_timeout == 30 and orig_socket_timeout preserved) then MIGRATED → restored; FAILING_OVER/FAILED_OVER same; relaxed_timeout=-1 config → MIGRATING leaves socket_timeout unchanged.
- MOVING (endpoint present): after triggering via SET key_receive_moving_0, assert: the in-use connection state (maintenance_state MOVING, host == "1.2.3.4", socket_timeout relaxed, should_reconnect() True or already reconnected — check contract), pool.connection_kwargs updated (host, socket_timeout, socket_connect_timeout, maintenance_state, maintenance_notification_hash, orig_* preserved), free connections disconnected, a NEW connection obtained during the moving window connects to 1.2.3.4 with relaxed timeouts and MOVING state (assert via recorded open_connection targets), and after ttl expiry (await asyncio.sleep(MOVING_TIMEOUT + 0.3)) kwargs and connection settings reverted to originals (state NONE, hash None).
- MOVING (null endpoint): timeouts relaxed + state MOVING but host unchanged; after ttl revert.
- RESP2: client with protocol=2 → no b"MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS" in any sent data, parser handler funcs are None... note an explicitly-enabled config with protocol=2 must raise RedisError at construction — test both (protocol=2 without config → fine, no handshake; protocol=2 + enabled config → RedisError).
- Disabled config: enabled=False → connection._maint_notifications_connection_handler is None, parser handlers None, no handshake bytes, socket_timeout untouched by pushes (and pushes won't be handled — skip sending pushes here; just assert clean lifecycle).

STYLE: follow the sync suite — test classes with docstrings, @pytest.mark.fixed_client, helper validation methods, setup via a base class with the open_connection patcher started/stopped around each test (use pytest fixtures or setup_method/teardown_method; note async fixtures work with pytest-asyncio auto mode). Always disconnect pools/clients at test end (await client.aclose()).

VERIFICATION before you finish:
- .venv/bin/python -m py_compile tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications.py tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications_handling.py (and helper) — must succeed.
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications.py tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications_handling.py --collect-only -q — on today's code this is EXPECTED to fail with ImportError/AttributeError on redis.asyncio.maint_notifications etc. Confirm the failure is exactly that (missing async feature), not a syntax/typo error in your files.
- Sanity-check every asserted behavior against the contract file rather than guessing.

Return: file list, a one-line-per-test-class summary of coverage, and the verification output (including the expected-failure proof).

- RESP2: client with protocol=2 → no b"MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS" in any sent data, parser handler funcs are None... note an explicitly-enabled config with protocol=2 must raise RedisError at construction — test both (protocol=2 without config → fine, no handshake; protocol=2 + enabled config → RedisError).
- Disabled config: enabled=False → connection._maint_notifications_connection_handler is None, parser handlers None, no handshake bytes, socket_timeout untouched by pushes (and pushes won't be handled — skip sending pushes here; just assert clean lifecycle).

STYLE: follow the sync suite — test classes with docstrings, @pytest.mark.fixed_client, helper validation methods, setup via a base class with the open_connection patcher started/stopped around each test (use pytest fixtures or setup_method/teardown_method; note async fixtures work with pytest-asyncio auto mode). Always disconnect pools/clients at test end (await client.aclose()).

VERIFICATION before you finish:
- .venv/bin/python -m py_compile tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications.py tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications_handling.py (and helper) — must succeed.
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications.py tests/maint_notifications/test_async_maint_notifications_handling.py --collect-only -q — on today's code this is EXPECTED to fail with ImportError/AttributeError on redis.asyncio.maint_notifications etc. Confirm the failure is exactly that (missing async feature), not a syntax/typo error in your files.
- Sanity-check every asserted behavior against the contract file rather than guessing.

Return: file list, a one-line-per-test-class summary of coverage, and the verification output (including the expected-failure proof).

# Contract: async standalone maintenance-notifications parity

Goal: bring `redis.asyncio.Redis`, async `Connection`, and async `ConnectionPool`/
`BlockingConnectionPool` to parity with the sync maintenance-notifications feature.
The sync implementation is the reference: `redis/maint_notifications.py` plus its
integration in `redis/connection.py` (classes `MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection`,
`MaintNotificationsAbstractConnectionPool`, and the hooks in `AbstractConnection`
/ `ConnectionPool`). Read those files before writing code. Scope is standalone only —
NO cluster/OSS-cluster support in async (omit everything named `oss_cluster_*` /
`OSSMaintNotificationsHandler` from the async surface).

Python floor is 3.10. The async parsers (`_AsyncRESP3Parser`, `_AsyncHiredisParser`)
already support maintenance push handlers via `AsyncPushNotificationsParser`
(`redis/_parsers/base.py`): they `await self.node_moving_push_handler_func(notification)`
and `await self.maintenance_push_handler_func(notification)`. Therefore all handler
entry points registered on async parsers MUST be `async def`.

Shared building blocks are reused, NOT duplicated: `MaintenanceState`, `EndpointType`,
`MaintNotificationsConfig`, all `*Notification` classes, `notification_types_mapping`
come from `redis.maint_notifications`.

## New module: `redis/asyncio/maint_notifications.py`

```python
class MaintNotificationsPoolHandler:
    def __init__(self, pool, config: MaintNotificationsConfig) -> None
        # attrs: self.pool, self.config,
        # self._processed_notifications = set(), self._lock = asyncio.Lock(),
        # self.connection = None, self._pending_tasks = set()  (strong refs to timer tasks)
    def set_connection(self, connection) -> None
    def get_handler_for_connection(self) -> "MaintNotificationsPoolHandler"
        # copy sharing _processed_notifications, _lock and _pending_tasks; connection=None
    def remove_expired_notifications(self) -> None      # sync, same logic as sync version
    async def handle_notification(self, notification) -> None
        # remove_expired_notifications(); NodeMovingNotification ->
        # await handle_node_moving_notification(...); else logger.error(...)
    async def handle_node_moving_notification(self, notification) -> None
    async def run_proactive_reconnect(self, moving_address_src=None) -> None
    async def handle_node_moved_notification(self, notification) -> None
```

`handle_node_moving_notification` mirrors the sync flow with these asyncio-specific
decisions:
- Early return when `not config.proactive_reconnect and not config.is_relaxed_timeouts_enabled()`.
- `async with self._lock:` — if notification already in `_processed_notifications`, return.
- Do NOT acquire `pool._lock` in the handler; the async pool methods acquire it
  internally (asyncio.Lock is not reentrant — this replaces the sync RLock nesting).
- `moving_address_src = self.connection.getpeername() if self.connection else None`
- `await self.pool.update_connections_settings(state=MaintenanceState.MOVING,
  maintenance_notification_hash=hash(notification), relaxed_timeout=config.relaxed_timeout,
  host_address=notification.new_node_host, matching_address=moving_address_src,
  matching_pattern="connected_address", update_notification_hash=True,
  include_free_connections=True)`
- proactive reconnect: if `notification.new_node_host is not None` →
  `await self.run_proactive_reconnect(moving_address_src)`; else schedule it at
  `ttl/2` (deferred-timer pattern below).
- Update `pool.update_connection_kwargs(**kwargs)` with exactly the sync kwargs:
  `maintenance_state=MaintenanceState.MOVING`, `maintenance_notification_hash=hash(n)`,
  plus `host=n.new_node_host` when not None, plus
  `socket_timeout=config.relaxed_timeout, socket_connect_timeout=config.relaxed_timeout`
  when `config.is_relaxed_timeouts_enabled()`.
- Schedule `handle_node_moved_notification(notification)` after `notification.ttl`.
- `await record_connection_handoff(pool_name=get_pool_name(self.pool))` using the ASYNC
  recorder `redis.asyncio.observability.recorder.record_connection_handoff` and
  `redis.observability.attributes.get_pool_name`.
- add notification to `_processed_notifications`.

Deferred-timer pattern (replaces `threading.Timer`):
```python
loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
def _fire():
    task = loop.create_task(coro_fn(*args))
    self._pending_tasks.add(task)
    task.add_done_callback(self._pending_tasks.discard)
timer_handle = loop.call_later(delay, _fire)
```

`run_proactive_reconnect` does NOT take `self._lock` (it is called while the lock is
already held inside `handle_node_moving_notification`; it is also safe unlocked when
fired from a timer):
```python
await self.pool.update_active_connections_for_reconnect(moving_address_src=moving_address_src)
await self.pool.disconnect_free_connections(moving_address_src=moving_address_src)
```

`handle_node_moved_notification` mirrors sync `handle_node_moved_notification`:
`async with self._lock:`; if
`pool.connection_kwargs.get("maintenance_notification_hash") == hash(notification)`,
revert kwargs to `maintenance_state=MaintenanceState.NONE`,
`maintenance_notification_hash=None`, `host=orig_host_address`,
`socket_timeout=orig_socket_timeout`, `socket_connect_timeout=orig_socket_connect_timeout`
(reading the `orig_*` values from pool.connection_kwargs, same keys as sync); then
`await pool.update_connections_settings(relaxed_timeout=-1, state=MaintenanceState.NONE,
maintenance_notification_hash=None, matching_notification_hash=hash(notification),
matching_pattern="notification_hash", update_notification_hash=True,
reset_relaxed_timeout=config.is_relaxed_timeouts_enabled(),
reset_host_address=config.proactive_reconnect, include_free_connections=True)`.

```python
class MaintNotificationsConnectionHandler:
    _NOTIFICATION_TYPES = {...}   # same dict as sync
    def __init__(self, connection, config) -> None
    async def handle_notification(self, notification) -> None
        # await record_maint_notification_count(...)  (async recorder; same args as sync)
        # dispatch to the two methods below, same logic as sync
    async def handle_maintenance_start_notification(self, maintenance_state, notification) -> None
    async def handle_maintenance_completed_notification(self, **kwargs) -> None
```
Logic is line-for-line the sync logic (guards on MOVING state and
`is_relaxed_timeouts_enabled()`, `connection.set_tmp_settings(tmp_relaxed_timeout=...)`,
`connection.update_current_socket_timeout(...)`, OSS-migrating id tracking via
`connection.add_maint_start_notification`, completed → `reset_tmp_settings(reset_relaxed_timeout=True)`,
`update_current_socket_timeout(-1)`, state NONE, `reset_received_notifications()`),
with `record_connection_relaxed_timeout` awaited from the async recorder. Reuse the
sync module's `add_debug_log_for_notification`? No — it touches `connection._sock`;
write a small local `_add_debug_log_for_notification(connection, notification)` that
logs connection repr + `connection.get_resolved_ip()` at DEBUG level.

## `redis/asyncio/connection.py`

### New mixin `MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection`

Must declare `__slots__ = ()` (async `AbstractConnection.__slots__` already contains
`"__dict__"`; a mixin without empty slots breaks class creation).

```python
class MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection:
    __slots__ = ()
    def __init__(self, maint_notifications_config, maint_notifications_pool_handler=None,
                 maintenance_state=MaintenanceState.NONE, maintenance_notification_hash=None,
                 orig_host_address=None, orig_socket_timeout=None,
                 orig_socket_connect_timeout=None, parser=None):
        # mirrors sync __init__ minus oss/event_dispatcher:
        # sets maint_notifications_config, maintenance_state, maintenance_notification_hash,
        # calls _configure_maintenance_notifications(...),
        # sets _processed_start_maint_notifications / _skipped_end_maint_notifications sets
    def _configure_maintenance_notifications(self, maint_notifications_pool_handler=None,
                 orig_host_address=None, orig_socket_timeout=None,
                 orig_socket_connect_timeout=None, parser=None):
        # mirror sync: disabled/None config -> handlers None and return.
        # parser required, must be _AsyncHiredisParser or _AsyncRESP3Parser else
        # RedisError (same messages as sync).
        # pool handler: copy via get_handler_for_connection(), set_connection(self),
        # parser.set_node_moving_push_handler(copy.handle_notification)
        # connection handler: MaintNotificationsConnectionHandler(self, config) (async one),
        # parser.set_maintenance_push_handler(handler.handle_notification)
        # store orig_host_address (default self.host), orig_socket_timeout
        # (default self.socket_timeout), orig_socket_connect_timeout
        # (default self.socket_connect_timeout) — same falsy-default semantics as sync.
    def set_maint_notifications_pool_handler_for_connection(self, pool_handler):  # mirror sync
    async def activate_maint_notifications_handling_if_enabled(self, check_health=True):
        # same guard as sync: protocol not in [2,"2"], config truthy and enabled,
        # _maint_notifications_connection_handler set, getattr(self,"host",None) is not None
    async def _enable_maintenance_notifications(self, maint_notifications_config, check_health=True):
        # endpoint_type = config.get_endpoint_type(self.host, self)
        # await self.send_command("CLIENT","MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS","ON",
        #                         "moving-endpoint-type", endpoint_type.value,
        #                         check_health=check_health)
        # response = await self.read_response(); != "OK" -> ResponseError(
        #   "The server doesn't support maintenance notifications")
        # except: ResponseError + enabled=="auto" -> logger.debug and continue; else raise
    def get_resolved_ip(self):    # same two-step logic as sync (socket getpeername then
                                  # socket.getaddrinfo fallback), using self._get_socket()
    def getpeername(self):        # peername[0] via _get_socket(), else None
    @property maintenance_state / setter (backed by self._maintenance_state)
    def add_maint_start_notification(self, id) / get_processed_start_notifications()
    def add_skipped_end_notification(self, id) / get_skipped_end_notifications()
    def reset_received_notifications(self)
    def update_current_socket_timeout(self, relaxed_timeout=None):
        # async flavor: timeout = relaxed_timeout if relaxed_timeout != -1 else self.socket_timeout
        # If a read is currently in flight (self._active_read_timeout_cm is not None),
        # reschedule its deadline:
        #   when = None if timeout is None else asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + timeout
        #   cm.reschedule(when)         (asyncio.Timeout, py>=3.11.3)
        #   fallback: cm.update(when) / no-op via hasattr checks for the async_timeout package
        # Swallow RuntimeError (no running loop) defensively.
    def set_tmp_settings(self, tmp_host_address=SENTINEL, tmp_relaxed_timeout=None)  # same as sync
    def reset_tmp_settings(self, reset_host_address=False, reset_relaxed_timeout=False)  # same as sync
    def _get_socket(self):
        # return self._writer.transport.get_extra_info("socket") if self._writer else None
        # (guard with try/except AttributeError -> None)
    def _get_parser(self): return self._parser
```

### `AbstractConnection` integration

- `class AbstractConnection(MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection):` (mixin base).
- New keyword params on `__init__` (all forwarded by `Connection`/`SSLConnection`/
  `UnixDomainSocketConnection` automatically via `**kwargs`):
  `maint_notifications_config=None, maint_notifications_pool_handler=None,
  maintenance_state=MaintenanceState.NONE, maintenance_notification_hash=None,
  orig_host_address=None, orig_socket_timeout=None, orig_socket_connect_timeout=None`.
- At the END of `__init__` (after `self.set_parser(parser_class)`):
  `self._active_read_timeout_cm = None` then
  `MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection.__init__(self, maint_notifications_config,
  maint_notifications_pool_handler, maintenance_state, maintenance_notification_hash,
  orig_host_address, orig_socket_timeout, orig_socket_connect_timeout, self._parser)`.
- `on_connect_check_health`: after the HELLO/auth `if/elif` chain and BEFORE the
  `# if a client_name is given` block insert:
  `await self.activate_maint_notifications_handling_if_enabled(check_health=check_health)`
  (mirrors sync ordering; the maint handshake does its own send+read and must complete
  before the pipelined SETINFO block).
- `read_response`: in the two `read_timeout is not None` branches, expose the running
  timeout context so `update_current_socket_timeout` can relax an in-flight read, but
  ONLY when maintenance notifications are active, so a disabled config leaves the
  lifecycle untouched:
  ```python
  async with async_timeout(read_timeout) as tm:
      if self._maint_notifications_connection_handler is not None:
          self._active_read_timeout_cm = tm
      try:
          response = await self._parser.read_response(...)
      finally:
          self._active_read_timeout_cm = None
  ```
- `disconnect()`: at the end of BOTH exit paths (the `asyncio.current_task() is None`
  early path and the normal path), mirror the sync post-close block:
  ```python
  if self.maintenance_state == MaintenanceState.MAINTENANCE:
      self.reset_tmp_settings(reset_relaxed_timeout=True)
      self.maintenance_state = MaintenanceState.NONE
      self.reset_received_notifications()
  ```
  (Factor into a small private helper.)

### New mixin `MaintNotificationsAbstractConnectionPool`

Mirror of the sync pool mixin minus OSS-cluster, adapted to asyncio.Lock:

```python
class MaintNotificationsAbstractConnectionPool:
    def __init__(self, maint_notifications_config=None, **kwargs):
        # is_protocol_supported = check_protocol_version(kwargs.get("protocol"), 3)
        # config None + supported -> MaintNotificationsConfig()
        # config truthy and config.enabled:
        #   not supported -> RedisError("Maintenance notifications handlers on connection
        #                                 are only supported with RESP version 3")
        #   self._maint_notifications_pool_handler = MaintNotificationsPoolHandler(self, config)
        #   self._update_connection_kwargs_for_maint_notifications(pool_handler)
        # else: self._maint_notifications_pool_handler = None
    def maint_notifications_enabled(self)                      # sync method, mirror
    async def update_maint_notifications_config(self, config)  # mirror sync minus OSS;
        # NOTE async because it disconnects free connections
    def _update_connection_kwargs_for_maint_notifications(self, maint_notifications_pool_handler=None)
        # mirror sync: sets maint_notifications_pool_handler + maint_notifications_config
        # in connection_kwargs, and orig_host_address / orig_socket_timeout
        # (default DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT) / orig_socket_connect_timeout
        # (default DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECT_TIMEOUT) if orig_host_address unset
    async def _update_maint_notifications_configs_for_connections(self, maint_notifications_pool_handler)
        # async with self._get_pool_lock():
        #   free: set_maint_notifications_pool_handler_for_connection + config + await conn.disconnect()
        #   in-use: same setter + config + conn.mark_for_reconnect()
    def _should_update_connection(self, conn, matching_pattern="connected_address",
                                  matching_address=None, matching_notification_hash=None)  # mirror
    def update_connection_settings(self, conn, state=None, maintenance_notification_hash=None,
                                   host_address=None, relaxed_timeout=None,
                                   update_notification_hash=False, reset_host_address=False,
                                   reset_relaxed_timeout=False)   # mirror sync (sync method)
    async def update_connections_settings(self, ...same signature as sync...)
        # async with self._get_pool_lock(): iterate in-use then (optionally) free
    def update_connection_kwargs(self, **kwargs)               # connection_kwargs.update
    async def update_active_connections_for_reconnect(self, moving_address_src=None)
        # async with lock: mark_for_reconnect for matching in-use conns
        # (REPLACES the existing no-arg method on ConnectionPool — keep behavior
        #  identical when called with no args: all in-use conns marked)
    async def disconnect_free_connections(self, moving_address_src=None)
        # async with lock: await conn.disconnect() for matching free conns
    def _get_pool_lock(self): return self._lock
    def _get_free_connections(self): return self._available_connections
    def _get_in_use_connections(self): return self._in_use_connections
```

### `ConnectionPool` integration

- `class ConnectionPool(MaintNotificationsAbstractConnectionPool, ConnectionPoolInterface)`.
- `__init__` gains `maint_notifications_config: Optional[MaintNotificationsConfig] = None`.
  Mirror sync guards: if `connection_class` is not a subclass of
  `MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection` or is a `UnixDomainSocketConnection` subclass →
  `enabled is True` raises `RedisError("Maintenance notifications are not supported with
  {connection_class}")`, otherwise force `maint_notifications_config =
  MaintNotificationsConfig(enabled=False)`. Call the mixin `__init__` AFTER
  `self._lock`/`self.connection_kwargs` exist, passing `**connection_kwargs`.
- Delete the old `update_active_connections_for_reconnect` (superseded by mixin).
- `ensure_connection`: change the has-data check to
  `if await connection.can_read() and not self.maint_notifications_enabled():`
  (mirror of sync `get_connection`).
- `BlockingConnectionPool` needs no extra changes (it shares `_available_connections`
  / `_in_use_connections` / `_lock`).

### `redis/asyncio/client.py`

`Redis.__init__` gains `maint_notifications_config: Optional[MaintNotificationsConfig] = None`.
Wiring mirrors sync `redis/client.py` (~lines 416-496):
- unix socket path: `enabled is True` → `RedisError("Maintenance notifications are not
  supported with Unix domain socket connections")`; kwargs get
  `maint_notifications_config=MaintNotificationsConfig(enabled=False)`.
- TCP path: `if maint_notifications_config and maint_notifications_config.enabled and
  not check_protocol_version(protocol, 3): raise RedisError("Maintenance notifications
  handlers on connection are only supported with RESP version 3")`; if config provided,
  add it to kwargs. (When None, ConnectionPool applies the RESP3 auto-default.)

Imports: `MaintNotificationsConfig` from `redis.maint_notifications`,
`check_protocol_version` from `redis.utils`.

## Invariants (both units must respect)

- RESP2 (`protocol=2`): no `CLIENT MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS` bytes on the wire ever; a
  RESP2 connection created through a pool has `_maint_notifications_connection_handler
  is None` (pool only auto-defaults the config when protocol resolves to 3; an
  explicitly enabled config + protocol 2 raises RedisError at pool/client construction).
- `MaintNotificationsConfig(enabled=False)`: parser handler funcs stay None, no
  handshake, no orig_* bookkeeping, `read_response` never records
  `_active_read_timeout_cm` (stays None).
- `enabled=True` + handshake non-OK response → `ResponseError` propagates out of
  `connect()`. `enabled="auto"` + error reply → warning-level tolerance: connection
  works normally.
- Defaults: pool/client with `protocol=3` (or default protocol, since
  `DEFAULT_RESP_VERSION == 3`) and no config → auto-created `MaintNotificationsConfig()`
  (enabled="auto") and handshake attempted.
- The public maintenance surface on async connections must match sync attribute names:
  `maintenance_state`, `maintenance_notification_hash`, `orig_host_address`,
  `orig_socket_timeout`, `orig_socket_connect_timeout`, `maint_notifications_config`,
  `set_tmp_settings`, `reset_tmp_settings`, `update_current_socket_timeout`,
  `getpeername`, `get_resolved_ip`, `mark_for_reconnect`/`should_reconnect`.
- Existing tests must stay green: `tests/maint_notifications/test_maint_notifications.py`,
  `tests/maint_notifications/test_maint_notifications_handling.py`, `tests/test_utils.py`.
- Do not touch `tests/test_scenario/`, `redis/connection.py`, `redis/maint_notifications.py`,
  `redis/client.py`, or `redis/_parsers/*`.

test_cluster_maint_notifications_handling.py
test_maint_notifications_handling.py
test_maint_notifications.py

"""
Async-compatible API for recording observability metrics.

This module provides an async-safe interface for Redis async client code to record
metrics without needing to know about OpenTelemetry internals. It reuses the same
RedisMetricsCollector and configuration as the sync recorder.

Usage in Redis async client code:
    from redis.asyncio.observability.recorder import record_operation_duration

    start_time = time.monotonic()
    # ... execute Redis command ...
    await record_operation_duration(
        command_name='SET',
        duration_seconds=time.monotonic() - start_time,
        server_address='localhost',
        server_port=6379,
        db_namespace='0',
        error=None
    )
"""

from datetime import datetime
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List, Optional

from redis.observability.attributes import (
    ConnectionState,
    GeoFailoverReason,
    PubSubDirection,
)
from redis.observability.metrics import CloseReason, RedisMetricsCollector
from redis.observability.providers import get_observability_instance
from redis.observability.registry import get_observables_registry_instance
from redis.utils import deprecated_function, str_if_bytes

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from redis.asyncio.connection import ConnectionPool
    from redis.asyncio.multidb.database import AsyncDatabase
    from redis.observability.config import OTelConfig

# Global metrics collector instance (lazy-initialized)
_async_metrics_collector: Optional[RedisMetricsCollector] = None

CONNECTION_COUNT_REGISTRY_KEY = "connection_count"


def _get_or_create_collector() -> Optional[RedisMetricsCollector]:
    """
    Get or create the global metrics collector.

---defaults---
"""Internal default helper functions shared across redis-py modules."""

import socket

# Connection defaults

DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT = 5  # 5s
DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECT_TIMEOUT = DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT
DEFAULT_SOCKET_READ_SIZE = 32768  # 32KB


def get_default_socket_keepalive_options() -> dict[int, int]:
    options = {}

    # Linux exposes TCP_KEEPIDLE; macOS exposes the equivalent TCP_KEEPALIVE.
    # Some platforms expose neither and only support SO_KEEPALIVE itself.
    tcp_keepidle = getattr(socket, "TCP_KEEPIDLE", None)
    if tcp_keepidle is None:
        tcp_keepidle = getattr(socket, "TCP_KEEPALIVE", None)
    if tcp_keepidle is not None:
        options[tcp_keepidle] = 30

    # Not every platform exposes interval/probe tuning constants.
    tcp_keepintvl = getattr(socket, "TCP_KEEPINTVL", None)
    if tcp_keepintvl is not None:
        options[tcp_keepintvl] = 5

    # Not every platform exposes interval/probe tuning constants.
    tcp_keepcnt = getattr(socket, "TCP_KEEPCNT", None)
    if tcp_keepcnt is not None:
        options[tcp_keepcnt] = 3

    return options


# Retry defaults
DEFAULT_RETRY_COUNT = 10
DEFAULT_RETRY_BASE = 0.01  # 10ms
DEFAULT_RETRY_CAP = 1  # 1s

708	        self.config = MaintNotificationsConfig(

710	        )

714	        """Test MaintNotificationsPoolHandler initialization."""
715	        assert self.handler.pool == self.mock_pool

720	    def test_remove_expired_notifications(self):
721	        """Test removal of expired notifications."""
722	        with patch("time.monotonic", return_value=1000):
723	            notification1 = NodeMovingNotification(
724	                id=1, new_node_host="host1", new_node_port=6379, ttl=10
725	            )
726	            notification2 = NodeMovingNotification(
727	                id=2, new_node_host="host2", new_node_port=6380, ttl=5
728	            )
729	            self.handler._processed_notifications.add(notification1)
730	            self.handler._processed_notifications.add(notification2)
731	
732	        # Move time forward but not enough to expire notification2 (expires at 1005)
733	        with patch("time.monotonic", return_value=1003):
734	            self.handler.remove_expired_notifications()
735	            assert notification1 in self.handler._processed_notifications
736	            assert (
737	                notification2 in self.handler._processed_notifications
738	            )  # Not expired yet
739	
740	        # Move time forward to expire notification2 but not notification1
741	        with patch("time.monotonic", return_value=1006):
742	            self.handler.remove_expired_notifications()
743	            assert notification1 in self.handler._processed_notifications
744	            assert (
745	                notification2 not in self.handler._processed_notifications
746	            )  # Now expired

1017	@pytest.mark.fixed_client
1018	class TestMaintNotificationsConfigEndpointType:
1019	    """Test MaintNotificationsConfig endpoint type functionality."""
1020	
1021	    def setup_method(self):
1022	        """Set up common mock classes for all tests."""
1023	
1024	        class MockSocket:
1025	            def __init__(self, resolved_ip):
1026	                self.resolved_ip = resolved_ip
1027	
1028	            def getpeername(self):
1029	                return (self.resolved_ip, 6379)
1030	
1031	        class MockConnection(MaintNotificationsAbstractConnection, ConnectionInterface):
1032	            def __init__(self, host, resolved_ip=None, is_ssl=False):
1033	                self.host = host
1034	                self.port = 6379
1035	                self._sock = MockSocket(resolved_ip) if resolved_ip else None
1036	                self.__class__.__name__ = "SSLConnection" if is_ssl else "Connection"
1037	
1038	            @property
1039	            def is_connected(self):
1040	                return self._sock is not None
1041	
1042	            def _get_socket(self):
1043	                return self._sock
1044	
1045	            def get_resolved_ip(self):
1046	                # Call the actual method from AbstractConnection
1047	                from redis.connection import AbstractConnection
1048	
1049	                return AbstractConnection.get_resolved_ip(self)  # type: ignore
1050	
1051	        self.MockSocket = MockSocket
1052	        self.MockConnection = MockConnection
1053	
1054	    def test_config_validation_valid_endpoint_types(self):
1055	        """Test that MaintNotificationsConfig accepts valid endpoint types."""

1065	    def test_endpoint_type_detection_ip_addresses(self):
1066	        """Test endpoint type detection for IP addresses."""
1067	        config = MaintNotificationsConfig()
1068	
1069	        # Test private IPv4 addresses
1070	        conn1 = self.MockConnection("192.168.1.1", resolved_ip="192.168.1.1")
1071	        assert (
1072	            config.get_endpoint_type("192.168.1.1", conn1) == EndpointType.INTERNAL_IP
1073	        )
1074	
1075	        # Test public IPv4 addresses
1076	        conn2 = self.MockConnection("8.8.8.8", resolved_ip="8.8.8.8")
1077	        assert config.get_endpoint_type("8.8.8.8", conn2) == EndpointType.EXTERNAL_IP
1078	
1079	        # Test IPv6 loopback
1080	        conn3 = self.MockConnection("::1")
1081	        assert config.get_endpoint_type("::1", conn3) == EndpointType.INTERNAL_IP
1082	
1083	        # Test IPv6 public address
1084	        conn4 = self.MockConnection("2001:4860:4860::8888")
1085	        assert (
1086	            config.get_endpoint_type("2001:4860:4860::8888", conn4)
1087	            == EndpointType.EXTERNAL_IP
1088	        )

1090	    def test_endpoint_type_detection_fqdn_with_resolved_ip(self):
1091	        """Test endpoint type detection for FQDNs with resolved IP addresses."""
1092	        config = MaintNotificationsConfig()
1093	
1094	        # Test FQDN resolving to private IP
1095	        conn1 = self.MockConnection(
1096	            "redis.internal.company.com", resolved_ip="192.168.1.1"
1097	        )
1098	        assert (
1099	            config.get_endpoint_type("redis.internal.company.com", conn1)
1100	            == EndpointType.INTERNAL_FQDN
1101	        )
1102	
1103	        # Test FQDN resolving to public IP

1105	        assert (

53	
54	    @staticmethod
55	    def validate_in_use_connections_state(
56	        in_use_connections: List[AbstractConnection],
57	        expected_state=MaintenanceState.NONE,
58	        expected_should_reconnect: Union[bool, str] = True,
59	        expected_host_address=DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
60	        expected_socket_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT,
61	        expected_socket_connect_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECT_TIMEOUT,
62	        expected_orig_host_address=DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
63	        expected_orig_socket_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT,
64	        expected_orig_socket_connect_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECT_TIMEOUT,
65	        expected_current_socket_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT,
66	        expected_current_peername=DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
67	    ):
68	        """Helper method to validate state of in-use connections."""
69	
70	        # validate in use connections are still working with set flag for reconnect
71	        # and timeout is updated
72	        for connection in in_use_connections:
73	            if expected_should_reconnect != "any":
74	                assert connection.should_reconnect() == expected_should_reconnect
75	            assert connection.host == expected_host_address
76	            assert connection.socket_timeout == expected_socket_timeout
77	            assert connection.socket_connect_timeout == expected_socket_connect_timeout
78	            assert connection.orig_host_address == expected_orig_host_address
79	            assert connection.orig_socket_timeout == expected_orig_socket_timeout
80	            assert (
81	                connection.orig_socket_connect_timeout
82	                == expected_orig_socket_connect_timeout
83	            )
84	            conn_socket = connection._get_socket()
85	            if conn_socket is not None:
86	                assert conn_socket.gettimeout() == expected_current_socket_timeout
87	                assert conn_socket.connected is True
88	                if expected_current_peername != "any":
89	                    assert conn_socket.getpeername()[0] == expected_current_peername
90	            assert connection.maintenance_state == expected_state
91	
92	    @staticmethod
93	    def validate_free_connections_state(
94	        pool,
95	        should_be_connected_count=0,
96	        connected_to_tmp_address=False,
97	        tmp_address=AFTER_MOVING_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
98	        expected_state=MaintenanceState.MOVING,
99	        expected_host_address=DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
100	        expected_socket_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT,
101	        expected_socket_connect_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECT_TIMEOUT,
102	        expected_orig_host_address=DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
103	        expected_orig_socket_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_TIMEOUT,
104	        expected_orig_socket_connect_timeout=DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECT_TIMEOUT,
105	    ):
106	        """Helper method to validate state of free/available connections."""
107	
108	        if isinstance(pool, BlockingConnectionPool):
109	            free_connections = [conn for conn in pool.pool.queue if conn is not None]
110	        elif isinstance(pool, ConnectionPool):
111	            free_connections = pool._available_connections
112	        else:
113	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported pool type: {type(pool)}")
114	
115	        connected_count = 0
116	        for connection in free_connections:
117	            assert connection.should_reconnect() is False
118	            assert connection.host == expected_host_address
119	            assert connection.socket_timeout == expected_socket_timeout
120	            assert connection.socket_connect_timeout == expected_socket_connect_timeout
121	            assert connection.orig_host_address == expected_orig_host_address
122	            assert connection.orig_socket_timeout == expected_orig_socket_timeout
123	            assert (
124	                connection.orig_socket_connect_timeout
125	                == expected_orig_socket_connect_timeout
126	            )
127	            assert connection.maintenance_state == expected_state
128	            if expected_state == MaintenanceState.NONE:
129	                assert connection.maintenance_notification_hash is None
130	
131	            conn_socket = connection._get_socket()
132	            if conn_socket is not None:
133	                assert conn_socket.connected is True
134	                if connected_to_tmp_address and tmp_address != "any":
135	                    assert conn_socket.getpeername()[0] == tmp_address
136	                connected_count += 1
137	        assert connected_count == should_be_connected_count

179	class MockSocket:
180	    """Mock socket that simulates Redis protocol responses."""
181	
182	    def __init__(self):
183	        self.connected = False
184	        self.address = None
185	        self.sent_data = []
186	        self.closed = False
187	        self.command_count = 0
188	        self.pending_responses = []
189	        # Track socket timeout changes for maintenance notifications validation
190	        self.timeout = None
191	        self.thread_timeouts = {}  # Track last applied timeout per thread
192	        self.moving_sent = False
193	
194	    def connect(self, address):
195	        """Simulate socket connection."""
196	        self.connected = True
197	        self.address = address
198	
199	    def send(self, data):
200	        """Simulate sending data to Redis."""
201	        if self.closed:
202	            raise ConnectionError("Socket is closed")
203	        self.sent_data.append(data)
204	
205	        # Analyze the command and prepare appropriate response
206	        if b"HELLO" in data:
207	            response = b"%7\r\n$6\r\nserver\r\n$5\r\nredis\r\n$7\r\nversion\r\n$5\r\n7.4.0\r\n$5\r\nproto\r\n:3\r\n$2\r\nid\r\n:1\r\n$4\r\nmode\r\n$10\r\nstandalone\r\n$4\r\nrole\r\n$6\r\nmaster\r\n$7\r\nmodules\r\n*0\r\n"
208	            self.pending_responses.append(response)
209	        elif b"MAINT_NOTIFICATIONS" in data and b"internal-ip" in data:
210	            # Simulate error response - activate it only for internal-ip tests
211	            response = b"+ERROR\r\n"
212	            self.pending_responses.append(response)
213	        elif b"SET" in data:
214	            response = b"+OK\r\n"
215	
216	            # Check if this is a key that should trigger a push message
217	            if b"key_receive_migrating_" in data or b"key_receive_migrating" in data:
218	                # MIGRATING push message before SET key_receive_migrating_X response
219	                # Format: >3\r\n$9\r\nMIGRATING\r\n:1\r\n:10\r\n (3 elements: MIGRATING, id, ttl)
220	                migrating_push = ">3\r\n$9\r\nMIGRATING\r\n:1\r\n:10\r\n"
221	                response = migrating_push.encode() + response
222	            elif b"key_receive_migrated_" in data or b"key_receive_migrated" in data:
223	                # MIGRATED push message before SET key_receive_migrated_X response
224	                # Format: >2\r\n$8\r\nMIGRATED\r\n:1\r\n (2 elements: MIGRATED, id)
225	                migrated_push = ">2\r\n$8\r\nMIGRATED\r\n:1\r\n"
226	                response = migrated_push.encode() + response
227	            elif (
228	                b"key_receive_failing_over_" in data
229	                or b"key_receive_failing_over" in data
230	            ):
231	                # FAILING_OVER push message before SET key_receive_failing_over_X response
232	                # Format: >3\r\n$12\r\nFAILING_OVER\r\n:1\r\n:10\r\n (3 elements: FAILING_OVER, id, ttl)
233	                failing_over_push = ">3\r\n$12\r\nFAILING_OVER\r\n:1\r\n:10\r\n"
234	
235	                response = failing_over_push.encode() + response
236	            elif (
237	                b"key_receive_failed_over_" in data
238	                or b"key_receive_failed_over" in data
239	            ):
240	                # FAILED_OVER push message before SET key_receive_failed_over_X response
241	                # Format: >2\r\n$11\r\nFAILED_OVER\r\n:1\r\n (2 elements: FAILED_OVER, id)
242	                failed_over_push = ">2\r\n$11\r\nFAILED_OVER\r\n:1\r\n"
243	                response = failed_over_push.encode() + response
244	            elif b"key_receive_moving_none_" in data:
245	                # MOVING push message before SET key_receive_moving_none_X response
246	                # Format: >4\r\n$6\r\nMOVING\r\n:1\r\n:1\r\n+null\r\n (4 elements: MOVING, id, ttl, null)
247	                # Note: Using + instead of $ to send as simple string instead of bulk string
248	                moving_push = f">4\r\n$6\r\nMOVING\r\n:1\r\n:{MOVING_TIMEOUT}\r\n_\r\n"
249	                response = moving_push.encode() + response
250	            elif b"key_receive_moving_" in data:
251	                # MOVING push message before SET key_receive_moving_X response
252	                # Format: >4\r\n$6\r\nMOVING\r\n:1\r\n:1\r\n+1.2.3.4:6379\r\n (4 elements: MOVING, id, ttl, host:port)
253	                # Note: Using + instead of $ to send as simple string instead of bulk string
254	                moving_push = f">4\r\n$6\r\nMOVING\r\n:1\r\n:{MOVING_TIMEOUT}\r\n+{AFTER_MOVING_ADDRESS}\r\n"
255	                response = moving_push.encode() + response
256	
257	            self.pending_responses.append(response)
258	        elif b"GET" in data:
259	            # Extract key and provide appropriate response
260	            if b"hello" in data:
261	                response = b"$5\r\nworld\r\n"
262	                self.pending_responses.append(response)
263	            # Handle specific keys used in tests
264	            elif b"key_receive_moving_0" in data:
265	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$8\r\nvalue3_0\r\n")
266	            elif b"key_receive_migrated_0" in data:
267	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$13\r\nmigrated_value\r\n")
268	            elif b"key_receive_migrating" in data:
269	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$6\r\nvalue2\r\n")
270	            elif b"key_receive_migrated" in data:
271	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$6\r\nvalue3\r\n")
272	            elif b"key_receive_failing_over" in data:
273	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$6\r\nvalue4\r\n")
274	            elif b"key_receive_failed_over" in data:
275	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$6\r\nvalue5\r\n")
276	            elif b"key1" in data:
277	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$6\r\nvalue1\r\n")
278	            else:
279	                self.pending_responses.append(b"$-1\r\n")  # NULL response
280	        else:
281	            self.pending_responses.append(b"+OK\r\n")  # Default response
282	
283	        self.command_count += 1
284	        return len(data)
285	
286	    def sendall(self, data):
287	        """Simulate sending all data to Redis."""
288	        return self.send(data)
289	
290	    def recv(self, bufsize):
291	        """Simulate receiving data from Redis."""
292	        if self.closed:
293	            raise ConnectionError("Socket is closed")
294	
295	        # Use pending responses that were prepared when commands were sent
296	        if self.pending_responses:
297	            response = self.pending_responses.pop(0)
298	            if b"MOVING" in response:
299	                self.moving_sent = True
300	            return response[:bufsize]  # Respect buffer size
301	        else:
302	            # No data available - this should block or raise an exception
303	            # For can_read checks, we should indicate no data is available
304	            import errno
305	
306	            raise BlockingIOError(errno.EAGAIN, "Resource temporarily unavailable")
307	
308	    def recv_into(self, buffer, nbytes=0):
309	        """
310	        Receive data from Redis and write it into the provided buffer.
311	        Returns the number of bytes written.
312	
313	        This method is used by the hiredis parser for efficient data reading.
314	        """
315	        if self.closed:
316	            raise ConnectionError("Socket is closed")
317	
318	        # Use pending responses that were prepared when commands were sent
319	        if self.pending_responses:
320	            response = self.pending_responses.pop(0)
321	            if b"MOVING" in response:
322	                self.moving_sent = True
323	
324	            # Determine how many bytes to write
325	            if nbytes == 0:
326	                nbytes = len(buffer)
327	
328	            # Write data into the buffer (up to nbytes or response length)
329	            bytes_to_write = min(len(response), nbytes, len(buffer))
330	            buffer[:bytes_to_write] = response[:bytes_to_write]
331	
332	            return bytes_to_write
333	        else:
334	            # No data available - this should block or raise an exception
335	            # For can_read checks, we should indicate no data is available
336	            import errno
337	
338	            raise BlockingIOError(errno.EAGAIN, "Resource temporarily unavailable")
339	
340	    def fileno(self):
341	        """Return a fake file descriptor for select/poll operations."""
342	        return 1  # Fake file descriptor
343	
344	    def close(self):
345	        """Simulate closing the socket."""
346	        self.closed = True
347	        self.connected = False
348	        self.address = None
349	        self.timeout = None
350	        self.thread_timeouts = {}
351	
352	    def settimeout(self, timeout):
353	        """Simulate setting socket timeout and track changes per thread."""
354	        self.timeout = timeout
355	
356	        # Track last applied timeout with thread_id information added
357	        thread_id = threading.current_thread().ident
358	        self.thread_timeouts[thread_id] = timeout
359	
360	    def gettimeout(self):
361	        """Simulate getting socket timeout."""
362	        return self.timeout
363	
364	    def setsockopt(self, level, optname, value):
365	        """Simulate setting socket options."""
366	        pass
367	
368	    def setblocking(self, blocking):
369	        pass
370	
371	    def getpeername(self):
372	        """Simulate getting peer name."""
373	        return self.address
374	
375	    def getsockname(self):
376	        """Simulate getting socket name."""
377	        return (self.address.split(":")[0], 12345)
378	
379	    def shutdown(self, how):
380	        """Simulate socket shutdown."""
381	        pass

384	class TestMaintenanceNotificationsBase:
385	    """Base class for maintenance notifications handling tests."""
386	
387	    def setup_method(self):
388	        """Set up test fixtures with mocked sockets."""
389	        self.mock_sockets = []
390	        self.original_socket = socket.socket
391	
392	        # Mock socket creation to return our mock sockets
393	        def mock_socket_factory(*args, **kwargs):
394	            mock_sock = MockSocket()
395	            self.mock_sockets.append(mock_sock)
396	            return mock_sock
397	
398	        self.socket_patcher = patch("socket.socket", side_effect=mock_socket_factory)
399	        self.socket_patcher.start()
400	
401	        # Mock select.select to simulate data availability for reading
402	        def mock_select(rlist, wlist, xlist, timeout=0):
403	            # Check if any of the sockets in rlist have data available
404	            ready_sockets = []
405	            for sock in rlist:
406	                if hasattr(sock, "connected") and sock.connected and not sock.closed:
407	                    # Only return socket as ready if it actually has data to read
408	                    if hasattr(sock, "pending_responses") and sock.pending_responses:
409	                        ready_sockets.append(sock)
410	                    # Don't return socket as ready just because it received commands
411	                    # Only when there are actual responses available
412	            return (ready_sockets, [], [])
413	
414	        self.select_patcher = patch("select.select", side_effect=mock_select)
415	        self.select_patcher.start()
416	
417	        # Create maintenance notifications config
418	        self.config = MaintNotificationsConfig(
419	            enabled=True, proactive_reconnect=True, relaxed_timeout=30
420	        )
421	
422	    def teardown_method(self):
423	        """Clean up test fixtures."""
424	        self.socket_patcher.stop()
425	        self.select_patcher.stop()
426	
427	    def _get_client(
428	        self,
429	        pool_class,
430	        connection_class=Connection,
431	        enable_cache=False,
432	        max_connections=10,
433	        maint_notifications_config=None,
434	    ):
435	        """Helper method to create a pool and Redis client with maintenance notifications configuration.
436	
437	        Args:
438	            pool_class: The connection pool class (ConnectionPool or BlockingConnectionPool)
439	            max_connections: Maximum number of connections in the pool (default: 10)
440	            maint_notifications_config: Optional MaintNotificationsConfig to use. If not provided,
441	                                    uses self.config from setup_method (default: None)
442	            setup_pool_handler: Whether to set up pool handler for moving notifications (default: False)
443	
444	        Returns:
445	            test_redis_client
446	        """
447	        config = (
448	            maint_notifications_config
449	            if maint_notifications_config is not None
450	            else self.config
451	        )
452	        pool_kwargs = {}
453	        if enable_cache:
454	            pool_kwargs = {"cache_config": CacheConfig()}
455	
456	        test_pool = pool_class(
457	            connection_class=connection_class,
458	            host=DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[0],
459	            port=int(DEFAULT_ADDRESS.split(":")[1]),
460	            max_connections=max_connections,
461	            protocol=3,  # Required for maintenance notifications
462	            maint_notifications_config=config,
463	            **pool_kwargs,
464	        )
465	        test_redis_client = Redis(connection_pool=test_pool)
466	
467	        return test_redis_client
